no-restricted-syntax.message: Don't bind an import to `module` — webpack passes the CommonJS module-wrapper object as a parameter to every bundled module, which shadows this binding in the minified production build (so `module.id` becomes a numeric chunk id, not your value). Alias it, e.g. `import { module as moduleTable }`. See 06_qa/audit-logs/2026-06-04-v171-polish-deploy.md.
Don't bind an import to a CommonJS global (`exports`/`require`/`__dirname`/`__filename`) — it collides with the bundler's module wrapper at runtime. Alias the import.
